_description: Historical case studies of companies responding to geopolitical events. Used by the RAG strategy recommender to ground recommendations in real precedents.

cases.action: Exited 19.75% Rosneft stake, taking $25.5B pre-tax write-down. CEO and former CEO resigned from Rosneft board.
cases.outcome: Clean exit but massive one-time loss. Stock recovered within 6 months as oil prices surged.

cases.action: Exited Sakhalin-2 LNG stake, shut Russian service stations, wound down all Russian operations. $3.9B pre-tax impairment.
cases.outcome: Record Q1 2022 earnings of $9.13B despite write-down, driven by oil price surge.

cases.action: Sold ~850 Russian restaurants to local licensee Alexander Govor, rebranded as Vkusno i Tochka. Retained right to repurchase within 15 years.
cases.outcome: $1.29B pre-tax charge. Russia/Ukraine were ~9% of systemwide revenue. Stock resilient as exit was already priced in.

cases.action: Suspended bookings to/from Russia. Rerouted vessels. Accepted short-term revenue loss from Russian trade.
cases.outcome: Overall business benefited as supply chain disruption tightened global shipping capacity and raised freight rates.
cases.cost: Lost Russia revenue, offset by rate surge

cases.action: Developed A800 and H800 compliance chips with reduced interconnect bandwidth designed to meet export control thresholds while still serving China market.
cases.outcome: Partially preserved China revenue with compliant chips. BIS later closed the loophole in October 2023.
cases.timeline: Controls Oct 2022, workaround chips by early 2023

cases.action: Complied with Dutch government restrictions on shipping EUV lithography machines to China. Continued DUV equipment sales under license.
cases.outcome: Lost access to China's most advanced chip manufacturing market. China accelerated domestic lithography development.
cases.cost: Lost EUV China sales (~15% of advanced equipment revenue)

cases.action: Rerouted all Red Sea traffic via Cape of Good Hope. Added 10-14 days transit time. Increased war risk insurance. Raised freight rates.
cases.outcome: Counterintuitively positive: tight capacity from longer routes raised freight rates, boosting Q1 2024 revenue.
cases.cost: Higher fuel and insurance costs, offset by rate increases

cases.action: Published statement on forced labor concerns. Decided to stop sourcing cotton from Xinjiang.
cases.outcome: Wiped from all Chinese e-commerce platforms. China revenue fell significantly. Recovery took 2+ years.
cases.cost: Lost China e-commerce presence, multi-year revenue decline

cases.action: Adopted 'brand of China, for China' messaging. Invested in local partnerships and Chinese athlete sponsorships.
cases.outcome: Recovered faster than H&M. Greater China revenue rebounded within 2 quarters.
cases.cost: Short-term brand damage, 15% China revenue dip

cases.action: Accelerated supply chain diversification to India and Vietnam. Began iPhone assembly at Tata facilities in India.
cases.outcome: Reduced China manufacturing concentration from ~95% to ~85% over 2 years. India now produces ~7% of iPhones.

cases.action: Relocated over 10,000 employees from Ukraine and Belarus to safer locations. Maintained business continuity through remote work activation.
cases.outcome: Stock fell 44% initially. $100M+ relocation costs. Successfully maintained most client relationships.

cases.action: Implemented 'Value at Stake' methodology to quantify geopolitical exposure across business units. Set up cross-functional task force for energy cost management.
cases.outcome: Identified and mitigated ~$2B in energy-related risk. Framework became permanent part of strategic planning.

cases.action: Established dedicated geopolitical risk team of 15+ analysts. Created scenario planning for critical mineral supply chains.
cases.outcome: Early warning on lithium and rare earth restrictions helped pre-position inventory and diversify suppliers.

cases.action: Moved ~$200B in assets and hundreds of staff from London to EU entities (primarily Frankfurt and Paris). Established EU banking subsidiary.
cases.outcome: Maintained EU market access. Compliance costs increased but revenue preserved.
cases.cost: Hundreds of millions in relocation and dual entity costs

cases.action: Suspended $20B Mozambique LNG project indefinitely after insurgent attack on Palma. Declared force majeure.
cases.outcome: $3.5B impairment. Project remains suspended. Insurgency partially contained but security insufficient for restart.

cases.action: Sold Myanmar operations (Telenor Myanmar) to M1 Group at distressed price after military junta demanded surveillance capabilities.
cases.outcome: $780M write-down. Exited a market of 18 million subscribers rather than comply with military surveillance demands.

cases.action: No strategic option available — Supreme Court ruled the mining concession unconstitutional. Forced to close Cobre Panama mine.
cases.outcome: Total loss of company's largest asset ($8.6B). Mine produced 1% of global copper. Stock collapsed.

cases.action: Pre-positioned oncology drug inventory ahead of potential tariff escalation. Built safety stock for critical APIs sourced from China.
cases.outcome: Avoided supply disruption when tariffs were imposed. Pre-positioning cost was minor vs potential stockout risk.

cases.action: Leveraged geopolitical muscle team to navigate M&A during pandemic uncertainty. Onboarded policy-makers for state actor approval.
cases.outcome: Successfully completed strategic acquisitions that competitors deferred due to uncertainty.

cases.action: Paid $4.4M Bitcoin ransom. Shut down pipeline for 6 days. DOJ later recovered $2.3M of the ransom.
cases.outcome: Fuel shortages across US East Coast. Led to executive order on critical infrastructure cybersecurity.

cases.action: Rebuilt IT infrastructure after NotPetya destroyed systems. Claimed $870M under property insurance. Filed landmark insurance lawsuit.
cases.outcome: Won $1.4B insurance settlement. Case established that cyberattacks can be covered under property policies.

cases.action: Paid $1.34B settlement to US authorities for processing dollar transactions on behalf of sanctioned entities. Restructured compliance department.
cases.outcome: Settlement resolved legal risk. Implemented real-time sanctions screening across all transactions.

cases.action: Pleaded guilty to violating US sanctions. Paid $8.9B penalty. One-year ban from dollar clearing.
cases.outcome: Largest bank penalty in history. Forced restructuring of compliance. Lost dollar clearing temporarily.

cases.action: Wound down Russia exposure, froze accounts of sanctioned entities, enhanced KYC for Russia-linked transactions.
cases.outcome: Avoided penalties through proactive compliance. Moderate revenue loss from Russia-linked business.

cases.action: Paid $1.1B combined settlement to US and UK authorities for processing transactions linked to Iran.
cases.outcome: Third sanctions-related fine for the bank. Led to CEO departure and major compliance overhaul.

cases.action: Delayed Russia exit, maintaining IT operations. Under scrutiny from ECB for slow wind-down.
cases.outcome: Reputational damage from delayed exit. Eventually reduced Russia workforce from 1,500 to under 500.

cases.action: Received €1.2B fine for transferring EU user data to US servers. Implemented EU data localization.
cases.outcome: Largest GDPR fine ever. Accelerated plans for EU-based data processing infrastructure.

cases.action: Received €746M GDPR fine from Luxembourg. Appealed the decision while updating ad targeting systems.
cases.outcome: Fine reduced on appeal. Shifted to first-party data strategy in EU markets.

cases.action: Received €1.49B fine for anti-competitive AdSense practices. Modified advertising contracts across EU.
cases.outcome: Third major EU fine. Total EU fines exceeded €8B. Restructured EU advertising business.

cases.action: No action available — CFIUS blocked the $117B hostile takeover bid from Broadcom on national security grounds.
cases.outcome: Remained independent. Stock initially fell but recovered as Qualcomm pursued own 5G strategy.

cases.event: India bans TikTok and 58 Chinese apps
cases.action: Attempted to comply with Indian data localization requirements. Offered to open source TikTok's algorithm.
cases.outcome: Ban remained permanent. Lost 200M Indian users — its largest market outside China.

cases.action: Built local data processing infrastructure in India. Invested in on-shore data centers.
cases.outcome: RBI banned new card issuance for 9 months due to non-compliance. Lost market share to RuPay.

cases.action: Developed HarmonyOS as Android replacement. Built semiconductor design capabilities to reduce reliance on US tools.
cases.outcome: Lost Google services access. Smartphone market share collapsed outside China but grew domestically.
cases.cost: Billions in R&D + lost international market share

cases.event: US CHIPS Act and chip export controls
cases.action: Committed to $40B Arizona fab construction. Began transferring advanced packaging capabilities to US.
cases.outcome: Maintained access to US equipment while diversifying manufacturing footprint. Arizona fab delayed.

cases.action: Accelerated domestic sourcing of photoresists, hydrogen fluoride, and fluorinated polyimide. Invested in Korean chemical suppliers.
cases.outcome: Reduced Japan dependency for critical materials from ~90% to ~50% within 2 years.

cases.action: Continued advancing chip fabrication using older-generation DUV equipment not covered by export controls.
cases.outcome: Achieved 7nm-equivalent production using multi-patterning DUV. Slower but functional workaround.

cases.action: Negotiated continued operations under new public-private partnership model. Accepted state majority ownership of future expansions.
cases.outcome: Maintained existing operations. New projects require joint ventures with state copper company Codelco.
cases.cost: Reduced future margins from Chilean operations

cases.action: Negotiated with DRC government on new royalty rates for cobalt mining. Accelerated recycling and alternative sourcing.
cases.outcome: Agreed to higher royalties. Maintained production but at lower margins.

cases.action: Secured alternative natural gas supplies. Invested in on-site renewable energy for German factories.
cases.outcome: Avoided production shutdowns. Energy costs increased ~40% temporarily.

cases.action: Permanently reduced Ludwigshafen operations. Shifted investment to China and US plants with cheaper energy.
cases.outcome: Cut 2,600 jobs at headquarters. Strategic pivot away from energy-expensive European production.

cases.action: Companies invested in diesel generators, solar panels, and battery storage. Some relocated operations.
cases.outcome: Manufacturing output fell 3.2%. Companies with backup power maintained operations at 2-3x energy cost.

cases.action: Redirected exports from China to US, UK, and Southeast Asian markets. Acquired US wine brands for market access.
cases.outcome: China revenue fell from $800M to near zero. Partial recovery through market diversification by 2023.

cases.action: Australian lobster exporters pivoted to selling live lobster through Canadian and US distributors at lower margins.
cases.outcome: Prices crashed initially. Gradual recovery as alternative markets absorbed supply.

cases.action: Absorbed some tariff costs, passed remainder to customers. Relocated some production from China-sourced components.
cases.outcome: Revenue dipped 3% on agricultural equipment. Farm sector customers delayed purchases due to retaliatory tariffs.

cases.action: Used Shanghai Gigafactory for EU exports to avoid US-origin tariffs. Benefited from lower Chinese production costs.
cases.outcome: Gained competitive advantage as EU tariffs targeted Chinese-brand EVs more heavily.

cases.action: CEO initially refused to comment on Xinjiang sourcing. Later quietly stopped sourcing Xinjiang cotton without public announcement.
cases.outcome: Avoided full boycott by staying neutral. Sales recovered faster than brands that took public positions.

cases.action: Maintained neutral stance. Temporarily reduced advertising in Hong Kong. Focused on mainland China brand strategy.
cases.outcome: Hong Kong revenue dipped but mainland China sales unaffected. Avoided boycott risk.

cases.action: KFC and Pizza Hut operations in Myanmar disrupted. Temporarily closed stores during protests and internet shutdowns.
cases.outcome: Minimal financial impact — Myanmar was <1% of revenue. Maintained operations through disruption.

cases.action: Converted Argentine peso holdings to USD as rapidly as possible. Repriced services in real-time to track devaluation.
cases.outcome: FX losses partially offset by fintech business growth. Maintained market leadership despite macro turmoil.

cases.action: Navigated changing regulatory environment. Maintained government relationships across political transitions.
cases.outcome: Operations continued with minor disruptions. License renewals delayed but eventually approved.

cases.action: Foreign investors hedged lira exposure through FX forwards. Some accelerated capital repatriation.
cases.outcome: Unhedged investors lost 40%+ on FX. Hedged investors preserved capital but at high hedging costs.

cases.action: Absorbed higher freight costs rather than raising prices. Increased inventory buffers for imported goods.
cases.outcome: Maintained price competitiveness. Margins compressed 0.3% from higher shipping costs.

cases.action: Rerouted all Red Sea traffic via Cape of Good Hope like competitors. Deployed larger vessels to offset longer routes.
cases.outcome: Higher fuel costs but surcharges more than offset them. Q1 2024 profits exceeded expectations.
cases.cost: Higher fuel and insurance, offset by surcharges

cases.action: Refused to pay ransom. Reverted to manual operations across 170 plants. Rebuilt IT from clean backups.
cases.outcome: Recovery took 3 months. Cost $70M. Became industry model for ransomware resilience.

cases.action: Paid $11M Bitcoin ransom after brief shutdown of US and Australian operations. Restored from backups in parallel.
cases.outcome: Paid ransom to speed recovery. All operations restored within 2 days. Led to Congressional scrutiny.

cases.action: Relocated employees from Ukraine. Maintained employment and pay for displaced workers. Set up remote work hubs in Poland and Romania.
cases.outcome: Retained key talent. Demonstrated employee care that strengthened recruitment brand.

cases.action: Expanded Frankfurt office to 600+ staff. Established EU booking entity. Moved €60B in assets.
cases.outcome: Maintained EU market access. Higher operating costs from dual London-Frankfurt operations.
cases.cost: Hundreds of millions in dual entity costs

cases.action: Restructured subsidies to comply with WTO ruling. Negotiated directly with US Trade Representative.
cases.outcome: US imposed 15% tariffs on Airbus jets. Eventually resolved in 2021 US-EU trade truce.
cases.cost: Lost price competitiveness vs Boeing in US market

cases.action: Sold 68% stake in AvtoVAZ (Lada) to Russian state entity NAMI for 1 ruble, with 6-year buyback option.
cases.outcome: €2.3B write-down. Preserved option to return. Lada continued production without Renault technology.

cases.action: Russian government seized Baltika brewery (Russia's largest). Carlsberg wrote off $1.5B Russian business.
cases.outcome: Total loss — assets expropriated. No compensation or buyback option.

cases.action: Suspended all operations in Russia. Cards issued by Russian banks ceased working outside Russia.
cases.outcome: Lost ~4% of global net revenue. Domestic Russian transactions switched to Mir payment system.

cases.action: Closed all operations and facilities in Ukraine. Incurred incremental costs due to the war including impairment charges.
cases.outcome: Operating income margin dropped from 15.3% to 11.2%. Recovered in 2023 after lapping war-related costs.

cases.action: Recorded $151M pre-tax charge for machinery write-downs, inventory losses, receivables allowances, and humanitarian efforts in Ukraine.
cases.outcome: Additional $53M charge in 2023. Maintained Russian operations (tobacco exempt from most sanctions).

cases.action: Evaluated Gillette brand for impairment due to reduced Russian market access and currency devaluation.
cases.outcome: Risk of further impairment if macroeconomic conditions worsen. Gillette brand under continued monitoring.

cases.action: Raised equipment prices 1-4% to offset tariff costs. Shifted some sourcing from China to other Asian suppliers.
cases.outcome: Revenue flat in 2019 vs growth in 2018. Construction equipment demand softened as customers delayed purchases.

cases.action: Initially benefited from tariffs protecting domestic manufacturing. Later hurt by retaliatory tariffs on exports and rising input costs.
cases.outcome: Stock rose 7% on tariff announcement. Net positive in 2018, turned negative by 2019 as costs rose.
cases.cost: $150M in higher raw material costs by 2019

cases.event: EU retaliatory tariffs on US goods
cases.action: Announced shift of EU-bound production from US to Thailand to avoid 31% EU retaliatory tariff.
cases.outcome: Faced political backlash from Trump administration. Completed Thailand factory. EU tariffs eventually suspended.
cases.cost: $45-55M per year in tariff costs before relocation

cases.action: Reduced forecast twice citing trade war uncertainty. Implemented cost cuts and restructuring.
cases.outcome: Stock dropped 13% on first guidance cut. Full-year revenue declined 1.9%.

cases.action: Lost Huawei as customer (was ~$8B/year). Applied for and received some export licenses for non-5G chips.
cases.outcome: Revenue dropped 12% in fiscal 2019. Partially recovered as other Chinese OEMs gained Huawei's market share.

cases.action: Initially halted all Huawei shipments. Later determined some products not covered by Entity List and resumed partial shipments.
cases.outcome: Found legal workaround for non-US-origin products. Resumed ~40% of Huawei shipments.
cases.cost: Temporary revenue loss, then partial recovery

cases.action: Complied with export controls restricting advanced chip sales to China. Applied for specific licenses for existing customers.
cases.outcome: China revenue declined from ~27% to ~15% of total. Accelerated pivot to US/EU government contracts.

cases.action: Ceased shipments of advanced semiconductor equipment to China. Maintained service contracts for existing installed base.
cases.outcome: China revenue fell from 33% to ~25% of total. Service revenue partially offset equipment loss.
cases.cost: ~$2.5B in China equipment revenue at risk

cases.action: Withdrew personnel from Chinese advanced fabs. Stopped equipment deliveries for sub-14nm production.
cases.outcome: China revenue dropped significantly. Pivoted to trailing-edge equipment which remained exportable.
cases.timeline: Controls Oct 2022, US person restriction immediate

cases.action: Unlike peers, did NOT fully exit Russia. Wrote down $3.7B but maintained Arctic LNG 2 stake. Stopped new investment.
cases.outcome: Faced criticism for staying. Arctic LNG 2 later sanctioned by US, forcing practical exit anyway.

cases.action: Sold Russian brewery operations to local buyer for €1 (effective loss of €300M net asset value).
cases.outcome: Clean exit. Accepted total loss rather than complex wind-down. Redirected capital to Vietnam and Africa.

cases.action: Closed all 17 Russian stores and ceased Russian manufacturing. Sold manufacturing assets. Maintained store leases initially.
cases.outcome: Lost ~€1.5B in annual Russian revenue (~3.5% of global). Repurposed supply chain capacity for other markets.

cases.action: Maintained Russian subsidiary despite pressure to exit. Reduced cross-border exposure. Set aside €1.3B for potential Russia losses.
cases.outcome: Russian subsidiary remained profitable despite sanctions. ECB ordered capital ring-fencing.

cases.action: Unable to return serviced Nordstream turbine to Russia due to Canadian sanctions. Caught between Russian demands and Western sanctions.
cases.outcome: Became center of geopolitical dispute. Turbine sat in Germany for months. Nordstream later sabotaged.

cases.action: Warned of delivery delays for furniture and home goods. Absorbed higher freight costs rather than raising prices immediately.
cases.outcome: Delivery times extended 2-4 weeks for some products. Stock levels maintained through increased inventory orders.

cases.action: Suspended Berlin Gigafactory production for 2 weeks due to component delays from Asia via Red Sea rerouting.
cases.outcome: Lost ~5,000 vehicles in production. Resumed after air-freighting critical components.

cases.action: Used lean inventory buffer stocks built after 2011 Fukushima. Pre-positioned chip inventory before shortage peaked.
cases.outcome: Lost less production than competitors. Still cut 40% of September 2021 production but recovered faster.

cases.action: Idled multiple plants. Shipped vehicles without certain chips, planning to retrofit later.
cases.outcome: Lost ~$2.5B in EBIT. Some vehicles sat in parking lots for months waiting for chips.

cases.action: Acquired Precor for $420M to gain US manufacturing. Invested $100M in air freight to reduce delivery times.
cases.outcome: Overspent on capacity as demand normalized post-COVID. Eventually reversed course with layoffs.

cases.action: Spent $1.5B+ on Project Texas — migrating all US user data to Oracle cloud infrastructure. Created US Data Security entity.
cases.outcome: Investment did not prevent congressional ban legislation. Offered unprecedented transparency but political opposition continued.

cases.action: Moved Chinese iCloud data to state-owned Guizhou-Cloud Big Data (GCBD). Stored encryption keys in China.
cases.outcome: Maintained App Store and iCloud access in China. Criticized by privacy advocates for key storage decision.

cases.action: Extended GDPR-level privacy protections to all users worldwide, not just EU. Invested in data classification tools.
cases.outcome: Turned compliance into competitive advantage. Azure gained EU government contracts due to trust.

cases.action: Withdrew from EU voluntary Code of Practice on Disinformation. Reduced trust and safety staff.
cases.outcome: EU launched formal investigation. Risk of fines up to 6% of global revenue.

cases.action: Discovered Russian-linked malware in Orion software update. Rebuilt software delivery pipeline. Hired CISO.
cases.outcome: Stock dropped 40%. Lost government contracts. SEC investigation. Eventually recovered through transparency.

cases.action: Paid $40M ransom — one of the largest known ransomware payments. Restored systems from clean backups in parallel.
cases.outcome: Operations restored within weeks. Disclosure of payment prompted industry debate about ransomware economics.

cases.action: Services went offline for 5 days. Reportedly paid ransom through intermediary to avoid OFAC sanctions issues.
cases.outcome: All services restored. No reported data theft. Used intermediary for payment due to Evil Corp sanctions.

cases.action: Idled two blast furnaces in Germany and one in Spain due to uneconomic energy costs. Shifted production to lower-cost facilities.
cases.outcome: European steel output fell 20%. Maintained profitability by shifting to Americas and scrap-based EAFs.

cases.action: Cut European ammonia production by 65% due to natural gas prices. Increased imports from lower-cost plants.
cases.outcome: Global fertilizer prices spiked. Yara profited despite lower European output due to high product prices.

cases.action: US-based producer benefited from European competitors shutting down. Increased nitrogen fertilizer exports.
cases.outcome: Record earnings in 2022. US natural gas advantage created windfall as European competitors idled.

cases.action: Reduced marketing spend in Hong Kong. Maintained operations despite transport disruptions.
cases.outcome: Hong Kong ride volume dropped during peak protests but was immaterial to global revenue.

cases.action: One franchise was accused of denying water to police. Starbucks maintained neutral corporate stance.
cases.outcome: Avoided mainland China boycott. Brief social media backlash subsided.

cases.action: Exited Myanmar reinsurance market. Withdrew from partnership with Myanmar military-linked entities.
cases.outcome: Clean exit from small market. Aligned with ESG commitments.

cases.action: Converted peso receivables to USD as rapidly as possible. Raised beer prices to match devaluation.
cases.outcome: FX translation loss in Q4 2023. Volume held as Argentine consumers maintained beer consumption.

cases.action: Filed arbitration against Mexico. Sold renewable energy assets at discount rather than face regulatory risk.
cases.outcome: Exited Mexican renewables market. Redirected investment to US and Europe.
cases.cost: Asset sale at below book value

cases.action: Relocated hundreds of employees from Ukraine to Poland, Romania, and other European offices. Maintained salaries.
cases.outcome: Client projects continued with minimal disruption. Demonstrated business continuity capability.

cases.action: Had significant R&D presence in Ukraine. Evacuated employees, maintained remote work, offered relocation.
cases.outcome: R&D output temporarily disrupted. Most Ukrainian employees continued working remotely or relocated.

cases.action: Relocated 3,000+ engineers from Ukraine. Set up new delivery centers in Poland, India, and Latin America.
cases.outcome: Maintained all client contracts. Accelerated geographic diversification of delivery capabilities.

cases.action: Stockpiled 6 months of medicines. Established EU batch release site in Sweden. Dual regulatory filings.
cases.outcome: No supply disruptions. Higher ongoing costs from dual regulatory compliance (UK MHRA + EU EMA).
cases.cost: Hundreds of millions in dual compliance

cases.action: Faced tariff risk if Sunderland plant failed EU rules of origin. Secured battery supply from Envision AESC UK gigafactory.
cases.outcome: Avoided tariffs by localizing battery supply. Sunderland plant investment confirmed for EVs.

cases.action: Russian government placed Danone's Russian dairy business under temporary state management.
cases.outcome: Lost control of Russian operations worth ~€600M. No compensation. Assets effectively expropriated.

cases.action: Wrote off €5.3B in Russian joint ventures with Gazprom. Exited all Russian partnerships.
cases.outcome: Largest write-off in company history. Delayed IPO plans. Merged with Harbour Energy instead.

cases.action: Absorbed potential 25% tariff risk on cognac and wine exports. Lobbied French government for trade truce.
cases.outcome: US-EU agreed to 5-year tariff truce in June 2021. Luxury goods exempted from most measures.
cases.cost: Lobbying costs, no material tariff impact

cases.action: Exited 30-year partnership with Rosneft. Wrote off $1.1B in Russian assets. Stopped new investments.
cases.outcome: Clean exit. Redirected capital to North Sea and US offshore wind.

cases.action: Suspended non-essential product sales in Russia (KitKat, Nescafé). Maintained baby food and medical nutrition citing humanitarian reasons.
cases.outcome: Faced boycott pressure for not fully exiting. Revenue impact limited — Russia was ~2% of sales.

cases.action: Continued selling essential products (food, hygiene) in Russia. Suspended media advertising and capital investment.
cases.outcome: Criticized by Ukrainian government. Russia was ~1.5% of revenue. Maintained market presence.

cases.action: Suspended Russian operations. Terminated Yeezy partnership separately. Closed 500+ Russian stores.
cases.outcome: Lost ~€250M Russia/CIS revenue. Combined with Yeezy termination, 2022 was a €700M revenue hit year.

cases.action: PwC separated from Russian member firm. 3,500 Russian employees transferred to new local firm.
cases.outcome: First Big Four firm to fully separate from Russia. Russian firm rebranded as Technologies of Trust.

cases.action: Stopped production in Russia and halted exports. Ukrainian wire harness suppliers disrupted, forcing German plant shutdowns.
cases.outcome: Several German plants idled 1-2 weeks due to missing Ukrainian-made parts. Diversified wire harness sourcing.

cases.action: Closed Russian office. Removed RT and Sputnik content. Maintained limited free tier access.
cases.outcome: Lost ~1.5M Russian subscribers. Minimal financial impact — Russia was <1% of revenue.

cases.action: Scaled infrastructure from 10M to 300M daily meeting participants. Offered free access to schools. Hired rapidly.
cases.outcome: Revenue grew 326% YoY. Market cap exceeded $100B. Became verb for video calling.

cases.action: Revenue dropped 72% in April 2020. Laid off 25% of staff. Refunded $1B in bookings. Pivoted to long-term stays.
cases.outcome: IPO'd December 2020 at $47B valuation. Long-term stay pivot became permanent strategy.

cases.action: Chartered private cargo ships to bypass port congestion. Hired 500K temporary workers. Expanded curbside pickup.
cases.outcome: E-commerce grew 79%. Successfully maintained supply through disruption. Retained logistics capabilities post-COVID.

cases.action: Grounded 600+ aircraft. Cut executive pay 50%. Furloughed 40K+ employees. Drew down $5.4B credit facility.
cases.outcome: Lost $12.4B in 2020. Received $5.6B in CARES Act payroll support. Full recovery by 2023.

cases.action: Partnered with BioNTech on mRNA vaccine. Invested $2B at risk without government R&D funding (Operation Warp Speed purchased doses only).
cases.outcome: Generated $37B in COVID vaccine revenue in 2022. Established mRNA platform for future vaccines.

cases.action: Designed mRNA vaccine within 2 days of receiving viral sequence. Scaled from zero commercial products to global manufacturing.
cases.outcome: Revenue went from $60M (2019) to $18.5B (2022). Validated entire mRNA technology platform.

cases.action: Requested tariff exemptions for Mac Pro components. Began planning supply chain diversification to India and Vietnam.
cases.outcome: Received some exemptions. Accelerated India manufacturing — first iPhones assembled at Foxconn Chennai.

cases.action: Warned consumers of price increases. Pressured Chinese suppliers to absorb costs. Accelerated sourcing from Vietnam, India.
cases.outcome: Passed some costs to consumers. Maintained price leadership. Sourcing shift partially permanent.
cases.cost: Higher input costs partially passed through

cases.action: Shifted production from China to Vietnam, Bangladesh, Indonesia. China sourcing fell from 25% to under 10%.
cases.outcome: Successfully diversified before tariffs fully hit. Lower-cost alternatives actually reduced COGS.

cases.event: China retaliatory tariffs on US pork
cases.action: Lost major Chinese pork export market due to retaliatory tariffs. Redirected exports to Japan, South Korea.
cases.outcome: US pork prices fell due to oversupply. Alternative markets partially offset but at lower margins.

cases.action: Halted shipments of advanced inspection equipment to Chinese fabs. Applied for export licenses for existing service contracts.
cases.outcome: China revenue dropped from 30% to ~20% of total. Process control equipment less impacted than lithography.

cases.action: Restricted EDA software access for Chinese companies designing advanced chips. Maintained legacy tool support.
cases.outcome: China revenue resilient in short term — legacy design tools not restricted. Long-term risk if controls expand.

cases.action: Complied with Japan's new export controls on 23 categories of semiconductor equipment to China.
cases.outcome: China was 25% of revenue. Lost advanced equipment sales but maintained trailing-edge tool business.

cases.event: US chip controls — memory in China
cases.action: Received one-year waiver to continue operating Dalian DRAM fab in China. Planned transition to legacy production.
cases.outcome: Waiver renewed annually. Shifted advanced production to South Korea. Dalian limited to legacy nodes.

cases.action: Used private fleet and pre-positioned inventory to buffer delays. Leveraged scale to negotiate priority shipping.
cases.outcome: Minimal consumer-facing impact. Higher logistics costs absorbed without price increases.

cases.action: Rerouted Asia-to-Europe shipments via Cape of Good Hope. Extended lead times for spring collection delivery.
cases.outcome: Some products arrived 2-3 weeks late. Fast fashion model strained by longer transit.

cases.action: Completely avoided Suez Canal. Rerouted all services via Cape of Good Hope. Raised freight rates significantly.
cases.outcome: Q1 2024 revenue surged 86%. Tight capacity from longer routes benefited carrier pricing.
cases.cost: Higher fuel costs, offset by rate surge

cases.action: Briefly attempted to transit Red Sea with naval escorts. Ultimately rerouted via Cape of Good Hope like competitors.
cases.outcome: Initially tried to maintain Suez transit for speed advantage. Abandoned after crew safety concerns.

cases.action: Curtailed aluminum production at Slovakian smelter due to electricity prices. Maintained Norwegian operations (hydro powered).
cases.outcome: European primary aluminum output fell 50%. Norway's renewable power became strategic advantage.

cases.action: Switched from gas to oil at German plants. Built LNG import terminal capacity. Locked in long-term energy contracts.
cases.outcome: Maintained production without shutdowns. Higher energy costs passed to specialty chemical customers.
cases.cost: Higher energy procurement + fuel switching costs

cases.action: Germany's largest gas importer forced to buy spot gas at extreme prices after Russia cut pipeline supplies.
cases.outcome: €40B government bailout and nationalization. Became poster child for European energy dependence.

cases.action: Accelerated switch from fossil fuels to alternative fuels in cement kilns. Invested in waste-to-energy.
cases.outcome: Reached 25% alternative fuel usage. Reduced gas dependence while cutting Scope 1 emissions.

cases.action: Attempted orderly wind-down of Russian consumer banking. Blocked by Russian government from selling retail operations.
cases.outcome: Stuck with Russian assets that couldn't be sold. Took $170M in quarterly provisions. Eventually transferred to local entity.

cases.action: Suspended operations in Russia. Cards issued by Russian banks ceased working internationally.
cases.outcome: Russia switched to Mir payment system. Lost ~2% of global transactions. Fully exited.

cases.action: Maintained Russian subsidiary despite pressure. Russian unit generated €2B+ in annual profit.
cases.outcome: Faced intense ECB/US regulatory pressure. Russian unit remained profitable but couldn't repatriate earnings.

cases.event: Ireland GDPR fine for privacy practices
cases.action: Received €225M GDPR fine for inadequate privacy disclosures. Updated privacy policy and transparency reports.
cases.outcome: Fine was second-largest GDPR penalty at the time. Led to industry-wide privacy notice overhauls.

cases.action: Fined by Italy (€20M), Greece (€20M), France (€20M), UK (£7.5M) for scraping facial images without consent.
cases.outcome: Effectively banned from operating in EU. Pivoted to US law enforcement as sole market.

cases.action: Redirected grain sourcing from Black Sea to South America. Increased soybean crush capacity in Argentina and Brazil.
cases.outcome: Record earnings as commodity trading profits surged. Benefited from supply dislocation.

cases.action: Redirected global grain flows. Increased origination from US and Brazil. Maintained customer supply commitments.
cases.outcome: Record $4.3B operating profit in 2022. Commodity trading desk profited from volatility.

cases.action: Expanded US manufacturing at Spartanburg, SC plant (largest BMW factory globally). Exported US-made SUVs to avoid tariffs.
cases.outcome: Spartanburg became export hub. Reduced tariff exposure by producing in the US for US market.

cases.action: Urgently qualified non-Japanese sources for photoresists and etching chemicals. Invested in domestic Korean suppliers.
cases.outcome: Successfully reduced Japan dependency. Korean chemical industry grew as Samsung directed investment.

cases.action: Paused US shipments from Belgian factory due to 25% tariff. Evaluated shifting US-bound production to South Carolina.
cases.outcome: US sales disrupted. Assessed reshoring of EX90 SUV production.
cases.cost: Lost US sales + potential reshoring investment

cases.event: US-China trade war impact on sourcing
cases.action: Accelerated shift from China to Vietnam manufacturing. Vietnam became largest sourcing country ahead of China.
cases.outcome: China fell from 25% to 15% of Nike production. Vietnam rose to 50%. Reduced tariff exposure.

cases.action: Worked with UN on Black Sea Grain Initiative. Maintained grain trading operations from alternative origins.
cases.outcome: Facilitated grain exports through humanitarian corridor. Record profits from commodity volatility.

cases.action: Suspended Myanmar operations during internet shutdowns and civil unrest.
cases.outcome: Myanmar was minimal market. Operations eventually resumed at reduced scale.

cases.action: Continued Argentine truck operations. Applied hyperinflationary accounting (IAS 29). Repriced vehicles frequently.
cases.outcome: Revenue in local terms grew massively but FX translation reduced dollar-denominated results.

cases.action: Hired Alison Levitt QC for independent review. Published Agenda for Change. Dropped non-compliant UK suppliers.
cases.outcome: Stock fell 42% in a week. Recovered over 18 months as compliance improvements verified.

cases.action: CEO resigned. Paid $30B+ in fines and settlements globally. Pivoted to electric vehicle strategy.
cases.outcome: Became world's largest EV manufacturer by volume by 2022. Scandal accelerated electrification.

cases.action: Grounded 737 MAX fleet worldwide after two fatal crashes. CEO fired. $18.7B in direct costs.
cases.outcome: 20-month grounding. Lost orders to Airbus. Reputation with regulators and airlines severely damaged.

cases.action: Quietly shifted sourcing away from Xinjiang cotton. Did not make public statement to avoid Chinese boycott.
cases.outcome: Avoided backlash in both Western and Chinese markets. Silent compliance strategy.

cases.action: Banned pro-Hong Kong esports player from competition. Faced massive Western backlash for censoring free speech.
cases.outcome: Reduced player ban. Boycott threats from US politicians. Tried to balance China market vs Western audience.

cases.action: Houston Rockets GM tweeted support for Hong Kong protests. NBA initially distanced, then defended free speech.
cases.outcome: China suspended NBA broadcasts for 18 months. Lost estimated $200M+ in Chinese revenue.

cases.action: Relocated employees from Eastern Europe. Activated business continuity plans for clients served from Ukraine.
cases.outcome: Minimal client impact. Demonstrated resilience of distributed delivery model.

cases.action: Ukrainian-founded company relocated hundreds of employees. Maintained product development from distributed locations.
cases.outcome: Zero service interruptions. CEO personally coordinated employee evacuations.

cases.action: Suspended ads in Russia. Expanded Trust & Safety team for conflict-related misinformation moderation.
cases.outcome: Maintained user trust during crisis. Content moderation costs increased.

cases.action: Had engineering team in Kyiv. Relocated employees to Poland and provided remote work support.
cases.outcome: Product development continued. Used crisis to permanently transition to distributed R&D model.

cases.action: Furloughed 67% of corporate staff. Closed 25% of hotels globally. Negotiated deferrals with franchise owners.
cases.outcome: RevPAR dropped 60% in 2020. Asset-light model protected balance sheet. Full recovery by mid-2022.

cases.action: Closed all theme parks globally. Laid off 28,000 employees. Accelerated Disney+ streaming launch.
cases.outcome: Parks revenue fell 85%. Disney+ reached 100M subscribers in 16 months. Streaming pivot became permanent.

cases.action: Pledged not to enforce COVID vaccine patents against manufacturers in 92 low-income countries.
cases.outcome: Selective IP waiver. Maintained patents in high-income markets. Addressed equity concerns while protecting core business.
cases.cost: Limited revenue impact (low-income markets were small)

cases.action: Stopped accepting bookings to/from Russia except food, medical, and humanitarian goods.
cases.outcome: Russia was ~2% of revenue. Compliance-driven decision maintained access to Western banking system.

cases.action: Insurers ceased covering Russian aviation, shipping, and energy. Left $10B+ in stranded Russian-leased aircraft.
cases.outcome: Aviation insurers face years of litigation over ~400 aircraft claimed by Russian airlines.

cases.action: Absorbed tariff costs for US-bound goods rather than raising prices. Maintained pricing consistency globally.
cases.outcome: Margin compression on US sales. Luxury demand proved resilient despite tariff-driven cost increases.

cases.action: Accelerated expansion of Taylor, Texas semiconductor fab. Shifted some appliance production to US.
cases.outcome: US manufacturing investment increased. Positioned as tariff-resilient supplier vs Chinese competitors.

cases.event: UK Supreme Court worker classification ruling
cases.action: Reclassified 70K UK drivers as workers (not self-employed). Provided minimum wage, holiday pay, pensions.
cases.outcome: UK costs increased 15-20%. Model for similar rulings in EU. Maintained contractor model in US.

cases.action: Spent $50M+ campaigning for Prop 22 exempting gig workers from AB5 employee classification law.
cases.outcome: Prop 22 passed. Gig companies maintained contractor model in California. Ruling later partially struck down.

cases.action: TotalEnergies capped fuel prices at French gas stations at cost as political gesture.
cases.outcome: Gained public goodwill. French government praised the move. Costs offset by record refining margins.

cases.action: Used Brexit as catalyst to pivot investment to Asia. Shifted $100B in assets to Hong Kong. Reduced European headcount.
cases.outcome: Asia now generates 65%+ of profit. Strategic realignment accelerated by Brexit uncertainty.

cases.action: Established Barclays Bank Ireland as EU hub. Moved €190B in assets. Hired 250+ staff in Dublin.
cases.outcome: Maintained EU market access. Dublin became European investment banking hub.

cases.action: Received less impact than NVIDIA — its MI250 data center GPU was below export control performance thresholds.
cases.outcome: Gained China market share as NVIDIA restricted. Later-generation MI300 also designed with export thresholds in mind.

cases.action: Suspended chip architecture license to Huawei per US Entity List. Later clarified which designs were UK-origin (not restricted).
cases.outcome: Partial resumption of service. Huawei developed some in-house alternatives but still relies on Arm ISA.

cases.action: Positioned mid-range chips not covered by export controls. Gained Huawei's lost Android chip market share.
cases.outcome: Became world's largest smartphone chip vendor. Benefited from Huawei's Kirin chip restrictions.

cases.action: Paid $22M ransom. Systems down for weeks, disrupting insurance claims for 1/3 of US healthcare.
cases.outcome: UnitedHealth took $872M charge. Congressional investigation. Largest healthcare data breach affecting 100M people.

cases.action: Refused to pay ransom. Shut down hotel and casino systems for 10 days. Operated manually (paper room keys, cash-only).
cases.outcome: $100M impact. Contrasted with Caesars which paid $15M ransom and recovered faster.

cases.action: Paid $15M ransom to prevent data leak. Systems recovered within days.
cases.outcome: Faster recovery than MGM (which refused to pay). Debate over whether paying was the right strategy.

cases.action: Patched zero-day vulnerability after mass exploitation. Over 2,500 organizations affected.
cases.outcome: Stock dropped 25%. Faced class-action lawsuits. Incident affected Shell, BBC, British Airways.

cases.action: Began removing expatriate staff from Sakhalin-1 oil project. Russia later nationalized the project through presidential decree.
cases.outcome: Lost $3.4B in Sakhalin assets. Russia transferred project to new state-controlled entity.

cases.action: Sold Russian dealer network and assembly operations to local investor. Maintained legal right to repurchase.
cases.outcome: €700M write-off. Clean exit with buyback option (similar to Renault strategy).

cases.action: Diversified sourcing away from China. Absorbed some tariff costs. Warned of price increases on certain categories.
cases.outcome: Successfully maintained price positioning. China sourcing fell from ~30% to ~25% of private label goods.

cases.action: Complied with expanded Dutch restrictions covering more DUV lithography models. Applied for exemptions for service of installed base.
cases.outcome: Lost additional China equipment revenue beyond EUV. Service revenue partially preserved through licenses.

cases.action: Ukrainian operations disrupted. Global demand for equipment surged as food security concerns drove investment.
cases.outcome: Record revenue in 2022-2023. Food security concerns accelerated precision agriculture adoption.
cases.cost: Ukraine operations disrupted, offset by global demand

cases.event: Ukraine grain crisis — Black Sea shipping
cases.action: Facilitated humanitarian grain corridors through Black Sea. Operated under enhanced security protocols.
cases.outcome: Maintained grain shipping capacity. Contributed to UN-brokered grain deal logistics.

cases.action: Terminated services for Russian-based merchants. Blocked payment processing for sanctioned entities.
cases.outcome: Minimal revenue impact — Russia was negligible market. Swift compliance maintained banking relationships.

cases.action: Suspended all deliveries to Russia. Recognized $95M provision for Russian receivables and inventory.
cases.outcome: Lost ~1% of revenue. Russian 5G rollout stalled without Western equipment.

cases.action: Positioned energy management solutions for European factories needing to optimize consumption.
cases.outcome: Record demand for energy management products. European energy crisis accelerated electrification orders.

cases.action: Disconnected 7 major Russian banks from SWIFT network per EU/US decision.
cases.outcome: Russia's major banks lost international wire transfer capability. Accelerated Chinese CIPS alternative.

cases.action: Faced tariff risk on UK-made vans if battery content didn't meet rules of origin. Lobbied for transition period.
cases.outcome: Secured transition period for EV battery content rules. Invested in UK battery supply chain.

cases.action: Maintained Simandou iron ore project in Guinea despite military coup. Negotiated with new military government.
cases.outcome: Simandou development continued. Military government honored existing mining agreements to maintain revenue.

cases.action: Wound down Russian metals and oil trading. Marked Russian assets to market, taking losses on stranded positions.
cases.outcome: Exited Russian commodity trade. Redirected to Middle East and African sources.

cases.action: Stopped all new business in Russia. Wrote down Russian wind farm service contracts.
cases.outcome: Minimal financial impact — Russia was small market for wind. Redirected to US IRA-driven demand.

cases.action: Suspended all services to Russia and Belarus. Rerouted European air freight around Russian airspace.
cases.outcome: Longer flight times for Asia-Europe routes increased fuel costs. Russia was <1% of revenue.
cases.cost: Higher fuel costs from airspace rerouting

cases.action: Suspended package delivery to Russia and Ukraine. Diverted cargo planes around closed Russian airspace.
cases.outcome: Asia-Europe transit times increased 2-4 hours. Minimal revenue impact.

cases.action: Shut down PayPal services in Russia entirely. Allowed withdrawals for a transition period.
cases.outcome: Lost Russian user base. Swift compliance maintained relationship with global banking partners.

cases.action: Suspended Russian service. Had previously refused to carry Russian state propaganda channels as required by law.
cases.outcome: Lost ~700K Russian subscribers. Compliance with Russian broadcast law was already a friction point.

cases.action: Stopped all vehicle deliveries and exports to Russia. Suspended Porsche Experience Center in Moscow.
cases.outcome: Russia was ~3% of deliveries. IPO proceeded successfully in Sept 2022 despite geopolitical turmoil.

cases.action: Restricted sales of advanced networking and AI accelerator chips to China. Maintained legacy product sales.
cases.outcome: China revenue declined. Pivoted to VMware acquisition and enterprise software strategy.

cases.action: Diversified suppliers beyond China. Invested in regional distribution centers to reduce single-point dependencies.
cases.outcome: Initially faced 2-3 month delays on popular items. Post-COVID supply chain more resilient.

cases.event: European energy crisis — data center costs
cases.action: Accelerated renewable energy procurement for European data centers. Signed long-term power purchase agreements.
cases.outcome: Locked in energy costs below spot. AWS European margins maintained despite energy price spikes.

cases.action: Temporarily laid off 900 workers at Canadian Windsor plant making minivans for US market due to tariff uncertainty.
cases.outcome: Evaluated shifting North American production mix. USMCA compliance became critical planning factor.
